warning: AI-assisted monitoring. Verify against official legislation before product approval, export, label declaration, or customer compliance confirmation.

rules.limit: Verification required via Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA) Online Database
rules.notes: The GSFA Online Database provides all provisions for food additives adopted by the Codex Alimentarius Commission, searchable by additive, functional class, and food category.
rules.evidenceQuote: The 'Codex General Standard for Food Additives' (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods

rules.limit: Verification required via General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995)
rules.notes: Codex establishes Maximum Levels (MLs) for contaminants, which are the maximum concentrations legally permitted in food or feed commodities to protect human health.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Codex maximum level (ML) for a contaminant in a food or feed commodity is the maximum concentration of that substance recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission to be legally permitted in that commodity.

rules.notes: Verification of approved food additives and their conditions of use is required against the Union list in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.
rules.evidenceQuote: This database can serve as a tool to inform about the food additives approved for use in food in the EU and their conditions of use. It is based on the Union list of food additives. This list is in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.

rules.parameter: Foodborne bacteria, toxins, and metabolites (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii, staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine)
rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Verification of food safety criteria and process hygiene criteria is required as laid down in Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005.
rules.evidenceQuote: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 on microbiological criteria for foods, applicable from 1 January 2006, lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites, such as Salmonella , Listeria monocytogenes , Enterobacter sakazakii , staphylococcal enterotoxins and histamine in specific foods.

rules.parameter: General requirements for sports supplementary foods
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 2209:2026 لائحة فنية خليجية --> الأغذية الرياضية التكميلية (GCC Technical Regulation --> Sports supplementary foods)
rules.notes: This regulation specifies requirements for sports supplementary foods, which may include additive limits, contaminant limits, and labelling. Specific details must be verified by consulting the full standard.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 2209:2026 لائحة فنية خليجية --> الأغذية الرياضية التكميلية

rules.foodCategory: Halal food, seasoning, snack food, processed foods
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 2055-1:2015 لائحة فنية خليجية --> الأغذية الحلال – الجزء الأول : الاشتراطات العامة للأغذية الحلال (GCC Technical Regulation --> Halal food - Part 1: General requirements for Halal food)
rules.notes: This regulation sets out general requirements for Halal food, including the Halal status of ingredients and additives. Specific details must be verified by consulting the full standard.
rules.evidenceQuote: الأغذية الحلال – الجزء الأول : الاشتراطات العامة للأغذية الحلال

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 2055-1:2015 لائحة فنية خليجية --> الأغذية الحلال – الجزء الأول : الاشتراطات العامة للأغذية الحلال (GCC Technical Regulation --> Halal food - Part 1: General requirements for Halal food)
rules.notes: This regulation sets out general requirements for Halal food, including purity requirements, which implies contaminant limits. Specific details must be verified by consulting the full standard.

rules.notes: This regulation specifies general labelling requirements for all prepackaged foodstuffs. Specific details must be verified by consulting the full standard.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية --> بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 2055-1:2015 لائحة فنية خليجية --> الأغذية الحلال – الجزء الأول : الاشتراطات العامة للأغذية الحلال (GCC Technical Regulation --> Halal food - Part 1: General requirements for Halal food)
rules.notes: This regulation sets out general requirements for Halal food, including labelling for Halal status. Specific details must be verified by consulting the full standard.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 2055-1:2015 لائحة فنية خليجية --> الأغذية الحلال – الجزء الأول : الاشتراطات العامة للأغذية الحلال

rules.parameter: Labelling requirements for sports supplementary foods
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 2209:2026 لائحة فنية خليجية --> الأغذية الرياضية التكميلية (GCC Technical Regulation --> Sports supplementary foods)
rules.notes: This regulation specifies requirements for sports supplementary foods, which may include additive limits, contaminant limits, and labelling. Specific details must be verified by consulting the full standard.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 2209:2026 لائحة فنية خليجية --> الأغذية الرياضية التكميلية

rules.foodCategory: Food products, seasoning, snack food, processed foods
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 150-1:2013 لائحة فنية خليجية --> فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الأول : فترات الصلاحية الإلزامية (GCC Technical Regulation --> Shelf life of food products - Part 1: Mandatory shelf life)

rules.foodCategory: Food products, seasoning, snack food, processed foods
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 150-2:2013 مواصفة قياسية خليجية --> فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الثاني : فترات الصلاحية الاختيارية (GCC Standard Specification --> Shelf life of food products - Part 2: Optional shelf life)
rules.notes: This standard provides guidelines for optional shelf life periods. Specific details must be verified by consulting the full standard.

rules.foodCategory: Halal food, seasoning, snack food, processed foods
rules.parameter: Hygienic practices and purity for Halal food
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 2055-1:2015 لائحة فنية خليجية --> الأغذية الحلال – الجزء الأول : الاشتراطات العامة للأغذية الحلال (GCC Technical Regulation --> Halal food - Part 1: General requirements for Halal food)
rules.notes: This regulation sets out general requirements for Halal food, including hygienic practices and purity, which implies microbiology and contaminant limits. Specific details must be verified by consulting the full standard.
rules.evidenceQuote: الأغذية الحلال – الجزء الأول : الاشتراطات العامة للأغذية الحلال

rules.foodCategory: Prepackaged food stuffs (including seasoning, snack food, processed foods)
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 9:2022 - Labeling of prepackaged food stuffs
rules.notes: All prepackaged food products must comply with the general labelling requirements specified in GSO 9:2022.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية --> بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 2055-1:2015 - HALAL FOOD - Part 1: General Requirements
rules.notes: Food products claiming to be Halal must comply with the general requirements for Halal food as specified in GSO 2055-1:2015.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 2055-1:2015 لائحة فنية خليجية --> الأغذية الحلال – الجزء الأول : الاشتراطات العامة للأغذية الحلال

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 150-1:2013 - Expiration dates for food products - Part 1: Mandatory expiration dates
rules.notes: Food products must comply with mandatory expiration date requirements as specified in GSO 150-1:2013.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 150-1:2013 لائحة فنية خليجية --> فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الأول : فترات الصلاحية الإلزامية

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 150-2:2013 - Expiration dates for food products - Part 2: Voluntary expiration dates
rules.notes: Food products may comply with voluntary expiration date guidelines as specified in GSO 150-2:2013.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 150-2:2013 مواصفة قياسية خليجية --> فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الثاني : فترات الصلاحية الاختيارية

rules.foodCategory: Foodstuffs, food ingredients (including seasoning, snack food, processed foods)
rules.notes: Specific microbiological limits for foodstuffs and food ingredients must be verified by consulting the full GSO 1016:2015 standard.
rules.evidenceQuote: This GSO technical regulation is concerned with microbiological limits for some foodstuffs intended for human consumption and for some food ingredients used in food industry.

rules.sourceTitle: SE Nomor 2 Tahun 2026 tentang Ketentuan Produksi, Importasi, Registrasi, dan Peredaran Bahan Tambahan Pangan Dinitrogen Monoksida (N2O)
rules.notes: Specific provisions for the production, import, registration, and circulation of Dinitrogen Monoxide (N2O) as a food additive must be verified.
rules.evidenceQuote: SE Nomor 2 Tahun 2026 Surat Edaran Nomor 2 Tahun 2026 tentang Ketentuan Produksi, Importasi, Registrasi, dan Peredaran Bahan Tambahan Pangan Dinitrogen Monoksida (N2O)

rules.parameter: Compliance with BPOM regulations for processed foods
rules.sourceTitle: Jaringan Dokumentasi dan Informasi Hukum Badan Pengawas Obat dan Makanan RI
rules.notes: There are 281 regulations concerning processed foods (including seasoning and snack products) that must be consulted for specific requirements.
rules.evidenceQuote: 281 Peraturan Pangan Olahan

rules.sourceTitle: PERKABPOM Nomor 34 Tahun 2019 Tentang Kategori Pangan
rules.notes: This regulation defines food categories, which is essential for determining applicable specific rules for seasoning and snack products.
rules.evidenceQuote: PERKABPOM Nomor 34 Tahun 2019 Tentang Kategori Pangan

rules.parameter: Additives (excluding natural flavoring agents and generally consumed substances used as additives)
rules.limit: Only those specified by MHLW as unlikely to cause harm are permitted. Verification required.
rules.evidenceQuote: Additives (excluding natural flavoring agents and things that have generally been served for human consumption, and are used as additives) and preparations and food containing additives must not be sold, or be produced, imported, processed, used, stored, or displayed for the purpose of sale, except for cases that the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies as unlikely to cause harm to human health by hearing the opinions of the Pharmaceutical Affairs and Food Sanitation Council.

rules.parameter: Apparatus, or containers and packaging which contains or bears toxic or harmful substances, or has a harmful effect on food/additives
rules.limit: Prohibited from sale, production, import for sale, or use in business.
rules.evidenceQuote: Apparatus, or containers and packaging which contains or bears toxic or harmful substances and is likely to cause harm to human health, or apparatus, or containers and packaging which comes into contact with food or additives and has a harmful effect on them and which is likely to cause harm to human health must not be sold, produced or imported for the purpose of sale, or used in business.

rules.parameter: Harmful contamination or foreign substances in food
rules.evidenceQuote: those which are likely to cause harm to human health due to contamination or the addition of foreign substances or on other grounds

rules.parameter: Methods of producing, processing, using, cooking, or preserving food or additives; ingredients of food or additives
rules.limit: Must conform to criteria/standards established by MHLW. Verification required.
rules.evidenceQuote: the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare may establish the criteria for the methods of producing, processing, using, cooking, or preserving food or additives... or may establish standards for the ingredients of food or additives... food or additives must not be produced, processed, used, cooked, or preserved using methods that do not conform to such criteria; food or additives that do not conform to such criteria must not be sold or imported; and food or additives that do not conform to such standards must not be produced, imported, processed, used, cooked, preserved, or sold.

rules.parameter: Residues of agricultural chemicals, feed additives, and pharmaceutical products used for animals in food
rules.limit: Must not exceed the quantity specified by MHLW as unlikely to cause harm to human health. Verification required.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food in which substances that are the ingredients of agricultural chemicals... feed additives... and pharmaceutical products... remain in a quantity exceeding the quantity that the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies as unlikely to cause harm to human health... must not be produced, imported, processed, used, cooked, preserved, for the purpose of sale, or sold

rules.parameter: Standards for apparatus, containers, packaging, raw materials thereof, and production methods
rules.limit: Must conform to criteria/standards established by MHLW. Verification required.
rules.evidenceQuote: the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare may establish standards for apparatus, or containers and packaging, or the raw materials thereof... or may establish the criteria for the production methods thereof... the apparatus, or containers and packaging that does not conform to the standards must not be sold, produced or imported for the purpose of sale, or used in business; raw materials that do not conform to the standards must not be used; and the apparatus, or containers and packaging must not be produced by methods that do not conform to the criteria.

rules.parameter: Toxic or harmful substances in food
rules.evidenceQuote: those which contain or bear toxic or harmful substances or are suspected to contain or bear such substances; provided, however, that this does not apply when the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies that the substances are unlikely to cause harm to human health

rules.parameter: Ensuring safety of food, additives, apparatus, containers and packaging; obtaining knowledge/skills; ensuring raw material safety; conducting voluntary inspections
rules.limit: Food business operators must endeavor to ensure safety and take necessary measures.
rules.evidenceQuote: A food business operator... on their own responsibility, must endeavor to ensure the safety of the food, additives, apparatus, or containers and packaging... and for that purpose, they must endeavor to obtain the knowledge and skills necessary to ensure the safety of food for sale, etc., ensure the safety of raw materials of food for sale, etc., conduct voluntary inspections of food for sale, etc., and take other necessary measures.

rules.parameter: Information that food containing MHLW-designated ingredients causes or may cause harm to human health
rules.limit: Business must notify the relevant prefectural governor, etc. without delay.
rules.evidenceQuote: A business that handles food containing any ingredient or material which requires special attention from the viewpoint of preventing food sanitation hazards and which has been designated by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare... upon obtaining information that any food containing designated ingredient, etc. handled by the business causes or may cause harm to human health, must notify the relevant prefectural governor, etc. of that information without delay pursuant to Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare Order.

rules.parameter: Record of sellers of food for sale or raw materials thereof
rules.limit: Food business operators must endeavor to make and retain records to prevent food sanitation hazards.
rules.evidenceQuote: A food business operator must endeavor to make a record of any necessary information such as the name of the person who has sold food for sale, etc. or raw materials thereof to the food business operator and retain the record, to the extent necessary for preventing food sanitation hazards resulting from food for sale, etc.

rules.parameter: Apparatus, and containers and packaging used in business
rules.evidenceQuote: Apparatus, and containers and packaging used in conducting business must be clean and sanitary.

rules.parameter: Collection, production, processing, use, cooking, storage, transport, display, and delivery of food or additives
rules.evidenceQuote: Food or additives which are meant to be sold... must be collected, produced, processed, used, cooked, stored, transported, displayed and delivered in a clean and sanitary manner.
those which are contaminated with pathogens or are suspected to be so, and which are likely to cause harm to human health

those which are rotten or have deteriorated, or are immature; provided, however, that this does not apply to those which are unlikely to cause harm to human health in general and are recognized as edible

rules.parameter: Combined Class II chemical preservatives (e.g., sorbic acid or benzoic acid)
rules.limit: Calculation required using SFA's Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives
rules.evidenceQuote: If two or more Class II chemical preservatives are to be used in the food (e.g. sorbic acid or benzoic acid), use the Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives to calculate the permitted levels that should be used.

rules.parameter: Compliance with regulatory limits for food additives
rules.evidenceQuote: All food businesses offering food for sale in Singapore must comply with the regulatory limits on food additives.

rules.limit: Only SFA-assessed and permitted additives allowed
rules.notes: Food additives are allowed when there is a technological justification and they do not present health risks to consumers.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives must be assessed for safety by SFA before they can be used in food sold in Singapore. Only food additives that have been assessed by SFA are permitted for use in food products sold in Singapore.

rules.notes: An application can be submitted to SFA to evaluate the use of an additive in a product intended for import or sale.
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food product contains an additive that is not permitted, do not import or sell it.

rules.parameter: Permitted food additives and their limits
rules.limit: Verification required using SFA's List of Permitted Food Additives or Food Additives Search tool, and Guidance Information on Requirements for Use of Food Additives.
rules.notes: Different limits apply, depending on the additive and the type of food products.
rules.evidenceQuote: Download the List of Permitted Food Additives or use the Food Additives Search tool to check if your additives are allowed. Different limits apply, depending on the additive and the type of food products.

rules.parameter: Compliance with regulatory limits for contaminants
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Food businesses must also comply with the Regulatory Limits for Food Additives.
rules.evidenceQuote: All food businesses must comply with the regulatory limits on incidental constituents i.e. contaminants in food.

rules.limit: Must be within SFA’s regulatory limits
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.evidenceQuote: Contaminants (also known as incidental constituents) in food sold in Singapore must be within SFA’s regulatory limits.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food or food products contain contaminants or incidental constituents that exceed the regulatory limits, do not import or sell them.

rules.limit: Verification required against SFA's Maximum Residue Limits (MRLs) list
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: These MRLs can be downloaded from the SFA website and will be included in future amendments of the Food Regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: SFA has also assessed the risk of the presence of other veterinary drug residues in various food commodities, and has established a list of maximum residue limits (MRLs) for veterinary drug residues for compliance by food businesses with effect from 1 May 2020.

rules.parameter: Specific contaminant limits (Microbiological, Heavy metals, Mycotoxins, Marine biotoxins, Veterinary drug residues, Pesticide residues, Other incidental constituents)
rules.limit: Verification required against SFA's specific documents/regulations
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Relevant documents include Microbiological standards, Heavy metals, Mycotoxins and Marine biotoxins, Veterinary drug residues, Pesticide residues, and Other incidental constituents.
rules.evidenceQuote: There are various limits imposed on different types of contaminants or incidental constituents in food. For more details, please refer to the relevant documents below: Microbiological standards, Heavy metals, Mycotoxins and Marine biotoxins, Veterinary drug residues, Pesticide residues, Other incidental constituents.

rules.foodCategory: Meat and food derived from meat
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: As defined under Regulation 29(3) of the Food Regulations, 'Veterinary drugs' include antibiotic and oestrogen.
rules.evidenceQuote: Regulation 33 of the Food Regulations prohibits the import or sale of meat and any food derived from meat which contains residues of the following compounds: diethylstibestrol (3, 4-bis(p-hydroxyphenyl)-3-hexene) hexoestrol (3, 4-bis(p-hydroxyphenyl)-n-hexane) dienoestrol (3, 4-bis(p-hydroxyphenyl)-2, 4-hexadiene)

rules.limit: Mandatory declaration in list of ingredients on food labels
rules.notes: Applies only to prepacked foods for sale in Singapore.
rules.evidenceQuote: All ingredients and additives used in food products must be declared in list of ingredients on food labels.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.evidenceQuote: Ready-to-eat (RTE) food are not required to be tested for total plate count (TPC).

rules.parameter: γ-Aminobutyric Acid (GABA) produced by microbial fermentation
rules.sourceTitle: Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient
rules.notes: Also includes labeling requirements. Relevant for seasoning, snack food, and processed foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient

rules.parameter: Cephalopods and cephalopod products (e.g., cuttlefish, squid, octopus), Spiral shells and Spiral shell products (e.g., escargot, mussel, clam, oyster, scallop), Seeds and seed products (e.g., sunflower seed, melon seed, excluding highly refined sunflower seed oil), Kiwis and kiwi products
rules.limit: Recommended declaration if present as an ingredient or food additive
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: These are recommended labeling allergens. Applies to seasoning, snack food, and processed foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: food allergen labeling is also recommended for the products that contain any of the following allergens as an ingredient or food additive

rules.parameter: Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish
rules.limit: Mandatory declaration if present as an ingredient or food additive
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: These are mandatory labeling allergens under Point 2 of the Regulation of Food Allergen Labeling. Applies to seasoning, snack food, and processed foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: For prepackaged foods, food allergen labeling is required for the products that contain any of the following 11 allergens as an ingredient or food additive

rules.limit: 10 or more milligrams per kilogram of food
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Mandatory declaration if the limit is met. Applies to seasoning, snack food, and processed foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: sulphite as the residue sulphur dioxide for 10 or more milligrams per kilogram of food.

rules.limit: Verification required against 'Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Foods'
rules.sourceTitle: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Foods
rules.notes: Applies to all foods, including seasoning, snack food, and processed foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Foods

rules.limit: Verification required against 'Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Animal Products'
rules.sourceTitle: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Animal Products
rules.notes: Relevant for processed foods containing animal products.
rules.evidenceQuote: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Animal Products

rules.limit: Verification required against 'Revised Regulations on Nutrition Claim for Prepackaged Food Products'
rules.sourceTitle: Revised Regulations on Nutrition Claim for Prepackaged Food Products
rules.notes: Applies to prepackaged foods, including seasoning, snack food, and processed foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: Revised Regulations on Nutrition Claim for Prepackaged Food Products

rules.parameter: Precautionary allergen labeling for cross-contamination risk
rules.limit: Suggested statement: 'The plant, equipment or production line used to produce the product is also used to process○○' or synonymous terms
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Suggested for situations where there is a risk of allergen cross-contamination. Applies to seasoning, snack food, and processed foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: the following statement of precautionary allergen labeling is suggested: “ The plant, equipment or production line used to produce the product is also used to process○○” or the synonymous terms.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.notes: Specific regulations for food additives must be verified with the Thai FDA.
rules.evidenceQuote: วัตถุเจือปนอาหาร

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.notes: Specific standards for contaminants in food must be verified with the Thai FDA.
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารที่มีสารปนเปื้อน

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks

rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารที่มีสารพิษ-ยาสัตว์ตกค้าง

rules.evidenceQuote: การแสดงฉลากอาหารและฉลากโภชนาการ

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks

rules.evidenceQuote: อาหารที่ห้ามผลิต นำเข้า หรือจำหน่าย

rules.parameter: Requirements for Food in Hermetically Sealed Containers
rules.sourceTitle: Ministry of Public Health Notification (No. 469) B.E. 2569
rules.notes: Specific requirements for food in hermetically sealed containers, including processing and labeling, must be verified.
rules.evidenceQuote: ประกาศกระทรวงสาธารณสุข (ฉบับที่ 469) พ.ศ. 2569 ออกตามความในพระราชบัญญัติอาหาร พ.ศ. 2522 เรื่อง อาหารในภาชนะบรรจุที่ปิดสนิท

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.notes: Specific standards for pathogenic microorganisms in food must be verified with the Thai FDA.
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารด้านจุลินทรีย์ที่ทำให้เกิดโรค

rules.notes: Manufacturers must verify the regulatory status and approved uses of food and color additives against FDA regulations and inventories.
rules.evidenceQuote: Approved Food and Color Additives and Petitions Under Review

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: Delisted from use in food, as listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR 81.10 and/or 81.30).
rules.evidenceQuote: Delisted color additives in FDA regulations (21 CFR 81.10 and/or 81.30) (labeled as "DELISTED").

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: Must comply with FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173, 73, 74). The Substances Added to Food inventory includes these, but full regulations must be consulted.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives and color additives that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173 and Parts 73, 74 respectively)

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: Must comply with FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 182 and 184). The Substances Added to Food inventory includes these, but full regulations must be consulted.
rules.evidenceQuote: Generally Recognized as Safe ("GRAS") substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 182 and 184).

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: Prohibited from use in food, as listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189). Some may have exceptions.
rules.evidenceQuote: Prohibited substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) as prohibited from use in food

rules.notes: The nine major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame.
rules.evidenceQuote: The major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame.

rules.notes: This is a guidance level.
rules.evidenceQuote: 3-Monochloropropane-1,2-Diol (3-MCPD) Acid-hydrolyzed protein and Asian-style sauces Guidance Level 1 ppm

rules.notes: This is an action level for total aflatoxins in general foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: Aflatoxins, total Foods Action Level 20 ppb

rules.notes: This is an action level for total aflatoxins.
rules.evidenceQuote: Aflatoxins, total Peanuts and peanut products Action Level 20 ppb

rules.notes: This is an action level for total aflatoxins.
rules.evidenceQuote: Aflatoxins, total Brazil nuts Action Level 20 ppb

rules.notes: This is an action level for total aflatoxins.
rules.evidenceQuote: Aflatoxins, total Pistachio nuts Action Level 20 ppb

rules.notes: Contaminant levels (tolerances, action levels, guidance levels, etc.) for poisonous or deleterious substances must be verified against the FDA Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool and referenced guidance documents/regulations (21 CFR parts 109 and 509).
rules.evidenceQuote: The Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool provides a consolidated list of these levels.

rules.notes: The FDA issued a final rule defining 'gluten-free' for food labeling. Compliance requirements also exist for fermented and hydrolyzed foods bearing the claim (effective August 12, 2020).
rules.evidenceQuote: On August 2, 2013, the FDA issued a final rule defining “gluten-free” for food labeling

rules.notes: Such statements are not required by law and should only be used to address unavoidable cross-contact after good manufacturing practices have been implemented. They must be truthful and not misleading.
rules.evidenceQuote: Such statements are not required by law and can be used to address unavoidable “cross-contact,” only if manufacturers have incorporated good manufacturing processes in their facility

rules.parameter: Allergen preventive controls and labeling accuracy
rules.notes: Facilities must put written procedures in place to control allergen cross-contact and ensure accurate allergen labeling, as per 21 CFR part 117 (CGMP & PC rule).
rules.evidenceQuote: The CGMP & PC rule includes requirements for allergen preventive controls to prevent allergen cross-contact in manufacturing and packaging and to prevent undeclared allergens.

rules.notes: Must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products due to being identified as allergenic substances.
rules.evidenceQuote: Color additives made from cochineal extract and carmine... must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products.

rules.notes: The food source of a major food allergen must appear in parentheses following the ingredient name OR immediately after or next to the ingredient list in a 'contains' statement. Specific types of tree nuts, fish, and crustacean shellfish must be declared.
rules.evidenceQuote: FALCPA requires that foods or ingredients that contain a "major food allergen" be specifically labeled with the name of the allergen source.

rules.notes: Required to be identified on labels due to potential hypersensitivity reactions.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA requires all products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 to identify it on their labels

rules.notes: Must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration is ≥10 ppm total sulfites.
rules.evidenceQuote: they must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration in the food is ≥10 parts per million total sulfites.
